text: Replace next_issue_id() full rglob with a last-id file

next_issue_id() only reads directory entries (filenames), not file contents — cheaper than initially noted. Still worth a last-id file once you hit a few hundred tickets, but low urgency. The id-field-removal idea was considered and rejected: makes raw JSON files less self-describing when read without filename context.

A cached last-id file should be treated as derived metadata, not the sole source of truth. It should be updated atomically and remain recoverable from the ticket filenames, or merges/corruption become harder to reason about.
disagree: no unneeded, derived files. I'd rather have a little extra cpu-cycles spent here.
